reviews.review_description:
I hope I can say this kind of stuff without it being insulting. I'm glad every time someone tries their hand at a VR game, but here we go. 

The textures are pastel, flat and shadowless. There's no depth of distance, so you can collide with the ground and never know you were close to it.

Flight is slow and interacting with the aircraft is a chore. Every time you get into the vehicle you'll be facing a different direction, your plane will be stuck in the ground, and despite it teleporting both it and you to what should be the ideal place in the cockpit, pulling back on the throttle will cause your body to clip into the seat behind you. 

The character designs seem bafoonish and low effort, with 3 second animations that repeat endlessly, all the while speaking in harmonics. It feels like really basic stuff, and calling it open world seems disingenuous as well. It's a small world that's initially limited by the incoming cloud, and though you can technically go anywhere within it, the boundaries are obvious and the incentives to follow a linear direction are clear when there are boosts guiding you. 

I don't know why this game looks so simple, with physics that only occasionally kick in, with textures and shadows that are non-existent, when so many games even going back to the Quest 1 did so much more and with so much less.

It's not a good game.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for taking the time to share your thoughts. While we're disappointed that Compass wasn't the right fit for you, we appreciate the detailed feedback.

Many of the elements you mentioned, from the visual style and character designs to the pacing of exploration, were intentional creative choices aimed at creating a unique atmosphere and sense of discovery. We understand that those choices won't resonate with everyone and that's okay!

We still appreciate you giving Compass a chance and sharing your experience. 😊

reviews.review_description:
I adore this game. Its so relaxing. The world is beautiful, the flying controls are perfect, the grappling and puzzles are fun. Its just a totally de-stressing experience. Im so glad I picked this up easily worth the full asking price.

If I had one complaint, it would be that its not available for the quest 2. I have a 3 (so glad i didnt get a 3s, the fov and extra resolution goes a long way on a game like this)  but I feel like this game should be available for more people to experience and although the game is beautiful im not exactly seeing visuals that wouldnt run on a 2. Im not a programer so maybe the large maps need the extra gpu and ram? 

Either way, everyone needs to give this game a try. Especially if u want to play something to calm you down.

developer_response.body:
Hi Kill3r_Chihulahula,

Thank you so much for your review about Compass. We really appreciate the kind words. 

We'd love for more people to be able to play Compass, but the Quest 2 unfortunately just can't keep up with what the game is doing under the hood. The combination of the large world, lighting system, shaders and scene complexity pushes the hardware beyond what we could comfortably support.

We optimized the game as much as possible, but getting it running on Quest 2 would have meant making major compromises to the experience.

Once again, thank you for your review, Scout!

reviews.review_description:
Note: After I left my initial review I requested a refund. Afterwards I found that I'd accidentally played it in low power mode. Because some of my complaints were regarding issues like resolution to be fair to the developers I repurchased the game to test it again in full power mode. (I realize I can't request a refund twice for one game but fair's fair.)

Unfortunately running in full power doesn't help, (and it possibly makes the performance worse), so I'm stuck with this.

The graphics looked simple, but charming in the promotional videos, however in the headset they look extremely dated. The models are basic, there are no dynamic shadows, and the textures look a bit muddy. I'm guessing the trailer is from the PCVR version. It's a Quest 3 exclusive yet the resolution isn't particularly sharp either. It doesn't feel like being in a vibrant cartoon world, it feels like looking at a cartoony flat screen game from decades ago.

And despite the Quest 1 era graphics the game doesn't even run smoothly which is astonishing. The frame rate drops when turning your ship which not only looks bad, it makes me queasy.

The performance is so poor I can't play it for more than 15 minutes or so without taking a break. I've been playing VR for almost a decade and can take almost anything without feeling ill.  Anything except vehicle sims with serious frame rate drops in the turns.

I think it actually played better in low power mode since that caps the frame rate and should make the frame rate more consistent. If the developers can't figure out how to optimize their code I'll try playing through it in low power mode.

The grappling mechanics feel as basic as the graphics. You're just pulling your viewpoint along and there's no sense of physics. The description says you "swing" but unless some mode unlocks that adds physics, you don't swing, you just drag yourself along. The vehicle gets around well enough, but again it doesn't seem to have any mass or momentum.

The "physics" are inconsistent. When grappling you float in the air yet while standing still in your ship you very slowly sink if you let go of the controls. This is very annoying during the character interactions since if you don't grip the controls, by the end of the conversation you'll be below the character talking to you. Oddly enough their ships don't sink.

The game claims to support standing play yet it glitches if you turn with your feet. Every time you get back to your ship you're facing a random direction, usually backwards or sideways.  Games like Walkabout Mini Golf got standing support right years ago. The Quest is a wireless headset, developers should test their games with natural turning if they claim to support standing or room scale.

The gameplay tasks are extremely simple but I assume the gameplay gets more complex and interesting as the game progresses.

Since I'm stuck with this I'm at the mercy of the developers to fix the performance issues and hopefully make it look like a Quest 3 game.

If the game gets substantial improvements I'll revisit this review but as of now I can't recommend this game to anyone.

developer_response.body:
Hi Jioxx,

We're sorry to hear that your experience didn't meet expectations. Building a large, seamless open-world VR adventure on standalone hardware comes with a lot of technical challenges and tradeoffs. We've spent a significant amount of time optimizing Compass and continue to do so, while balancing the demands of an expansive open world, flying mechanics, visuals and overall gameplay experience.

That said, we've noted your feedback regarding performance during turns, standing-play orientation issues and some of the physics behaviors you described. While certain aspects of the movement and grappling systems are intentional design choices, we understand they may not match everyone's expectations. We'll be continuing to evaluate player feedback as we improve the game.

Thanks again for giving Compass an honest review and we hope you can give our game a second chance in the future! ☺️

reviews.review_description: It's like nothing I have played before.  It took me awhile to figure out exactly what I was supposed to be doing but if you stick with it, explore and talk to all the NPCs you will start to understand how things work.  Mechanics work well although it has a slight learning curve.  Graphics are nice yet simple.  Music fits the game well.  All and all it's a great game especially for what the asking price is.  Definitely recommend!

developer_response.body:
Hey APEboys,

We're very happy to hear that Compass feels unlike anything you've played before. Thank you so much for your review and for recommending our game! 😄

reviews.review_title: A game with genuine soul — highly recomm
reviews.review_description:
Compass is a gorgeous exploration game where getting lost isn't a bug, it's the whole point. Half the time you have no idea where you're going, and somehow that's completely charming.
As you explore, you'll stumble across ruins filled with puzzles that get progressively more interesting the deeper you go. The world looks fantastic, the ally designs are unique and memorable, and the soundtrack immediately stood out — seriously, the music is incredible.
There are some hazards to watch out for — mines and lasers that can literally blow up your ship — which keeps things tense without feeling unfair. My only real complaint is that the ship feels a little slow at the start, though upgrades later on do help with that.
Overall, Compass is one of those rare games that feels like it has a real identity. If you're someone who looks for games with "soul," this is absolutely one of them.
10/10 — this one gets my seal of approval.

developer_response.body:
Hi NinjaGuyVR,

Thank you so much for the kind words and thoughtful review! We're happy that the sense of exploration, the world and the soundtrack resonated with you. It means a lot to hear Compass described as a game with "soul" beaucause that's exactly the experience we hoped to create. Thanks for joining us on the adventure, Scout!

reviews.review_description: This is one cool game with realistic flight physics in a wonderful artistic world. It also has grappling hook mechanics. It does not cause dizziness. It is fun family like and meditative. Deserves more likes for developing such a large open world. Can add a variety of other puzzle mechanics to spice things up. Big kudos to the developers!!!!

developer_response.body:
Hey Shayor,

Thank you so much for the wonderful review! We're very happy to hear you enjoyed the flying, grappling, art style and relaxing atmosphere of Compass.

It means a lot to us that you recognized the work that went into creating such a large world. Thanks again for the kind words and for taking the time to share your thoughts.

reviews.review_description: Why aren't there more games like this on the Meta Store? Kudos for the art direction! It's truly a beautiful game set in an interesting and quite unique world. It's cozy and makes you want to explore. The characters are really cute (Nym <3) and the background music is excellent. I had to get used to the driving controls (I'm terrible at them), but after a few drives, it becomes quite intuitive. I'm running it perfectly on my Quest 3. Good job, Trebuchet.

developer_response.body:
Hi TheGreggyMax,

Thank you very much for this review, we're really happy to see that you like Compass this much!

reviews.review_description:
Compass is a hidden gem in the VR world—a breathtaking open-world flying adventure that blends charm, mystery, and pure joy into one unforgettable experience. The Trebuchet Studio team has crafted something truly special with a unique art style that pops in VR, making every flight feel like a dream.
You’ll upgrade your plane, tackle puzzles and mysteries using grapples in risky environments, and uncover a world that feels alive with side missions, NPCs, and hidden shrines (yes, it’s very Zelda-like in the best way). The cozy yet adventurous vibe keeps you engaged without ever feeling overwhelming.
The UI is flawless, especially the plane customization—getting that perfect height and handling just feels so satisfying. And with no pressure to rush, it’s the kind of game you can pick up anytime for a quick escape or a deep dive.
If you love open-world adventures with a twist, Compass is a must-play—especially on Meta Store. Five stars, no question. 🌟✈️

developer_response.body:
Hey metaquesta99,

Wow, thank you for the incredible review! It means a lot to us to see Compass described as a hidden gem. Creating a world that feels adventurous, cozy and rewarding to discover was one of our biggest goals, so it's wonderful to hear that experience came through!

Thank you for taking the time to share your thoughts and for recommending Compass. Happy flying, Scout! ☺️

reviews.review_description: This game is soo chill and good. Well worth my money. The only problem I have had with this game is that the frames sometimes drop but other than that the game is amazing.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review! We're glad you're enjoying the adventure and feel Compass was worth your time and money. Thanks for flying with us, Scout!

reviews.review_description: Very good game ..i just hope the devs can puts some  hints  in this game  .

developer_response.body:
Hi 51963,

Thank you for the review! We're happy you're enjoying Compass and appreciate the 4 stars.

We also appreciate the feedback about hints. Compass is designed around exploration and discovery, but it's helpful to hear when players would like a little more guidance along the way.

Thanks for taking the time to share your thoughts, Scout!

reviews.review_title: A unique addition for the Q3
reviews.review_description: This is a beautiful, cool little flying game with windlands vibes, give it a go 👍

developer_response.body:
Hey Leadboots1,

Thank you very much for the review about Compass. We really appreciate that!

reviews.review_description:
Exploring, flying and discovery is the main activity. An open-world aerial navigation game with light puzzle-solving and grabbing mechanics.

I enjoyed every minute, I was flying through those pastel- colored skies. And still do.

Hope to see achievements in the meta app soon.  Have had a few glitches and a crash, but this didn’t stop me from playing.

developer_response.body:
Hi POWERGIRL.BE,

Thank you for the review! We're delighted to hear you've been enjoying the exploration, discovery and those pastel-colored skies. Knowing that you're still happily flying around the world means a lot to us.

Thanks for taking the time to share your experience and happy flying, Scout!
